author: y4rr
content: Plan: unary_map is the foundation function that many ops (clamp, abs, sqrt, neg forward, silu/elu/mish CPU fallback, etc.) route through. Currently it downloads GPU tensors to CPU. The fix: when the input is CUDA, check if there's a matching GPU kernel for the operation. However, unary_map takes an arbitrary closure — we can't dispatch a closure to GPU. Two approaches: (1) Add a generic GPU unary kernel that takes a precompiled PTX function pointer — complex, needs JIT. (2) Instead of fixing unary_map itself, ensure all callers that receive GPU tensors have their own GPU dispatch before reaching unary_map — which we've already been doing (silu, elu, mish, gelu, etc. now dispatch before falling through). The remaining callers that hit unary_map with GPU tensors are: clamp, abs (forward), sqrt (forward), neg (forward via the forward function). Let me check which of these already have GPU kernels.
